nodes.title: 📋 Batch Video Caption — Instructions
widgets_values:
BATCH VIDEO CAPTIONING  v0.8.0
https://github.com/hodgemann/ComfyUI-BatchFolderTools
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

REQUIRED:  ComfyUI-QwenVL-Mod
https://github.com/huchukato/ComfyUI-QwenVL-Mod

REQUIRED:  opencv-python
pip install opencv-python  (if not already installed)

SETUP:
1. Paste the full path to your video folder into the folder_path field.
2. folder_path is wired to the Save node, so caption .txt files save right next to your videos.  Disconnect the wire and type a different path if you want them elsewhere.
3. Set mode to 'sequential' on the Video Loader.
4. Choose your frame_mode.  'evenly_spaced' with frame_count=16 is a good default — the VLM sees 16 frames spread across the full duration.  Use 'first_frame' for a thumbnail description.

RUNNING:
5. Click Queue Prompt once.  The Queue Next node handles the rest.  Console shows ✅ DONE when complete.
6. To stop mid-run, press Escape or click Cancel.

RESUMING AN INTERRUPTED BATCH:
If the run gets interrupted (crash, power loss, you stopped it), set skip_captioned to true on the Video Loader and click Queue Prompt.  The loader checks for an existing .txt/.caption/.cap file next to each video and skips any that already have one — zero GPU cost for already-done files.  It picks up right where it left off.

FRAME MODES:
• first_frame — one frame from the start
• last_frame — one frame from the end
• all_frames — every frame (watch VRAM!)
• evenly_spaced — N frames sampled evenly (default 16)
• frame_range — contiguous range by index

FOLDER_PATH FORMATS:
• WSL:     /mnt/c/Users/me/videos  or  /mnt/d/datasets
• Linux:   /home/user/videos  or  /data/training_set
• macOS:   /Users/me/videos  or  /Volumes/External
• Windows: C:\Users\me\videos  or  D:\datasets
• Network shares work if mounted and readable by the OS.

VIDEO FORMATS: MP4, AVI, MOV, MKV, WebM, FLV, WMV, M4V

OUTPUTS:
The Video Loader outputs fps and duration_seconds alongside frames.  You may see a console warning about 'fps frames per second but no video_metadata was provided' — this is harmless and comes from QwenVL's internal processing.

Describe this video in comprehensive detail for AI video generation model training. Write a single flowing paragraph covering only what is visibly present: the main subject or subjects including their appearance, clothing, and distinguishing features; all actions, movements, and gestures that occur throughout the video described in chronological order; the setting and environment; camera work such as static shots, panning, tracking, zooming, handheld movement, or shot transitions; lighting direction, quality, color temperature, and any changes across the video; the dominant color palette and tonal range; the medium and style such as live-action footage, animation, screen recording, slow motion, time-lapse, or color grading. If there is visible text, titles, or on-screen graphics, describe them. Be precise about the temporal flow — what happens first, next, and last. Only describe what you can see. Do not mention the absence of anything.
